labels.name: Input
labels.desc: Data coming in from a user or source
labels.example:
Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);
String name = sc.nextLine();

labels.name: Loop
labels.desc: Something that repeats — processing each line, each item

labels.name: File read
labels.desc: Reading data in from a file on disk

labels.name: File write
labels.desc: Writing data out to a file on disk

labels.name: Exception
labels.desc: An error condition that must be caught or handled

labels.name: List
labels.desc: A dynamic, ordered collection that can grow
labels.example:
List<Product> items = new ArrayList<>();
items.add(new Product(...));

labels.name: Map
labels.desc: A collection that stores values looked up by a key
labels.example:
Map<String, Product> lookup = new HashMap<>();
lookup.put(product.getName(), product);

labels.name: Parse
labels.desc: Breaking a raw string into meaningful fields — splitting on a delimiter
labels.example:
String[] parts = line.split("\\|");
double price = Double.parseDouble(parts[2]);

prompts.title: Load Employees from File
prompts.statement: A company stores employee records in a text file — one per line, fields separated by a pipe character. Read the file, parse each record into an employee object, and display each one. If the file cannot be read, the program should report the error rather than crashing.

expectedTags.phrase: stores employee records in a text file
expectedTags.explanation: The employee data lives in a file on disk, so the program must open and read that file to access the records.

expectedTags.phrase: fields separated by a pipe character
expectedTags.explanation: Each line must be split on the pipe delimiter to extract the individual fields from the raw text.

expectedTags.phrase: display each one
expectedTags.explanation: Every employee record must be printed to the screen, which requires iterating over each line and producing visible output for each.

expectedTags.phrase: report the error rather than crashing
expectedTags.explanation: A file-read failure is an error condition that must be caught and surfaced to the user gracefully instead of terminating the program.

prompts.title: Payroll File Processor
prompts.statement: HR needs a payroll report generated from a raw data file. Ask the user for the input file path and the output file path. Read each record, compute gross pay, and write the formatted results to the output file. If either file can't be opened, the program should show a helpful error.

expectedTags.phrase: Ask the user for the input file path and the output file path
expectedTags.explanation: The user must supply both file paths at runtime, making this a direct input operation.

codeSnippets.input:
System.out.print("Input file: "); String inputFile = scanner.nextLine();
System.out.print("Output file: "); String outputFile = scanner.nextLine();

expectedTags.phrase: Read each record
expectedTags.explanation: The program opens the input file on disk and processes every record in it one by one, which requires both file reading and iteration.

expectedTags.phrase: compute gross pay
expectedTags.explanation: Gross pay can only be calculated after splitting each raw line into its individual fields such as hours and rate.

expectedTags.phrase: write the formatted results to the output file
expectedTags.explanation: Each computed result is written to a file on disk and also represents the formatted output of the payroll report.

expectedTags.phrase: program should show a helpful error
expectedTags.explanation: An inability to open either file is an error condition that must be caught and communicated to the user rather than left unhandled.

prompts.title: Load Product Catalog
prompts.statement: A store keeps its product catalog in a flat file. Build an in-memory list of products from it so the rest of the application can work with them. Each line holds a product's SKU, name, and price separated by pipe characters. The program reads through every line in the file.

expectedTags.phrase: keeps its product catalog in a flat file
expectedTags.explanation: The catalog data is stored on disk in a file, so the program must open and read that file to access the products.

expectedTags.phrase: Build an in-memory list of products
expectedTags.explanation: The loaded products are collected into a dynamic, ordered list structure held in memory for the rest of the application to use.

codeSnippets.list:
List<Product> products = new ArrayList<>();
products.add(new Product(parts[0], parts[1], Double.parseDouble(parts[2])));

expectedTags.phrase: separated by pipe characters
expectedTags.explanation: Each raw line must be split on the pipe delimiter to extract the SKU, name, and price as separate fields.

codeSnippets.parse:
String[] parts = line.split("\\|");
new Product(parts[0], parts[1], Double.parseDouble(parts[2]))

expectedTags.phrase: The program reads through every line in the file
expectedTags.explanation: Processing the entire catalog requires iterating over each line from start to finish.

prompts.title: Product Name Lookup
prompts.statement: The store's search feature lets customers look up any product by its exact name. Load all products into a lookup map keyed by product name so any name can be found instantly, then let the user type a name and display what they find.

expectedTags.phrase: lookup map keyed by product name
expectedTags.explanation: Products are stored in a map indexed by name for instant retrieval; populating that map requires reading the product file from disk, iterating through every line, and splitting each one to extract the name and price.

codeSnippets.parse:
String[] parts = line.split("\\|");
Product p = new Product(parts[0], parts[1], Double.parseDouble(parts[2]));

expectedTags.phrase: let the user type a name
expectedTags.explanation: The customer provides the search term at runtime, which is a direct user input operation.

codeSnippets.input:
System.out.print("Search by name: ");
String name = scanner.nextLine();

expectedTags.phrase: display what they find
expectedTags.explanation: The result — either the matching product or a not-found message — must be printed, and the program must branch based on whether a match exists.

prompts.title: Store Navigation Menu
prompts.statement: A shopping app needs a home screen where customers decide what to do next. Show a menu with at least three options — viewing products, viewing the cart, and exiting — and navigate to the right screen based on what they choose. Keep the menu active until they exit.

expectedTags.phrase: Show a menu
expectedTags.explanation: The menu options must be printed to the screen so the customer can see what choices are available.

expectedTags.phrase: customers decide what to do next
expectedTags.explanation: The customer's selection is entered at runtime, making this a user input operation.

expectedTags.phrase: navigate to the right screen based on what they choose
expectedTags.explanation: The program must branch to a different action depending on which option the customer selected.

expectedTags.phrase: Keep the menu active until they exit
expectedTags.explanation: The menu must repeatedly redisplay after each action, which requires a loop that continues until the customer chooses to leave.

prompts.title: Product Search
prompts.statement: Shoppers want to find products without scrolling through the whole catalog. Give them three ways to narrow results: by name, by price range, or by department. Display everything that matches.

expectedTags.phrase: Give them three ways to narrow results: by name, by price range, or by department
expectedTags.explanation: The shopper selects a search type at runtime (input), and the program must branch differently depending on which filter they chose (condition).

codeSnippets.input:
System.out.println("Search by: 1. Name  2. Price range  3. Department");
int option = scanner.nextInt();

expectedTags.phrase: without scrolling through the whole catalog
expectedTags.explanation: Finding matches still requires iterating over every product in the catalog to check each one against the chosen filter.

expectedTags.phrase: Display everything that matches
expectedTags.explanation: Matching products are collected into a list and then printed so the shopper can see all results at once.

codeSnippets.list:
List<Product> results = new ArrayList<>();
results.add(p); // add each matching product

prompts.title: Shopping Cart
prompts.statement: A shopper wants to add items to their cart and check what they've selected. Let them pick a product from the list, add it, then view the cart with a running total at any time.

expectedTags.phrase: Let them pick a product from the list
expectedTags.explanation: The shopper enters a product identifier at runtime (input), and finding it requires iterating through the product list to locate a match (loop).

codeSnippets.input:
System.out.print("Enter SKU to add: ");
String sku = scanner.nextLine();

expectedTags.phrase: add it
expectedTags.explanation: The selected product is appended to the cart, which is a dynamic list that grows with each addition.

expectedTags.phrase: view the cart with a running total at any time
expectedTags.explanation: Displaying the cart requires iterating over every item to print it and accumulate the total, producing visible output for the shopper.

prompts.title: Safe File Read
prompts.statement: A program reads a data file at startup. If the file can't be found — maybe the user moved it or mistyped the name — the program should display a clear message rather than crashing.

expectedTags.phrase: reads a data file at startup
expectedTags.explanation: The program opens a file from disk at launch and processes it line by line, requiring both a file-read operation and iteration through its contents.

expectedTags.phrase: If the file can't be found
expectedTags.explanation: A missing file is an error condition that the program must detect and branch on rather than letting it propagate unchecked.

expectedTags.phrase: display a clear message rather than crashing
expectedTags.explanation: The error response is a printed message shown to the user, which is an output operation.

codeSnippets.output: System.out.println("File not found. Please check the filename and try again.");
